Poetry.author: John Keats

works.theory_depth: This ode explores the speaker's desire to escape the suffering of life and attain a state of transcendence, highlighting the tension between the beauty of nature and the harsh realities of human existence.

works.theory_depth: This ode examines the relationship between art and reality, suggesting that beauty is a universal truth that transcends the fleeting nature of human experience.

works.theory_depth: This ode examines the speaker's experience of melancholy and sorrow, while also celebrating the beauty of nature and the human experience.

works.theory_depth: This collection of letters explores the speaker's love for Fanny Brawne, highlighting the power of love and desire to transcend the fleeting nature of human experience.

Literary Criticism.author: Northrop Frye

works.title: The Archetypes of Literature
themes: The role of myth in literature
quotes: The mythopoeic imagination is the source of all great literature.
The archetypes are the universal symbols that recur in all great literature.
works.theory_depth: Frye's theory of archetypal criticism posits that all great literature is rooted in a shared mythological and symbolic language, which he calls the 'mythopoeic imagination'. This theory has been influential in shaping the field of literary criticism and has been applied to a wide range of literary works.

style_innovations: Use of mythological and archetypal analysis in literary criticism

legacy.posthumous_notes: Frye's work continues to be widely read and studied today, and his influence can be seen in many areas of literary criticism and theory.

works.title: The Republic

works.title: The Intentional Fallacy
quotes: The design of the poem is the only design.
The poem itself cannot be verified or falsified.
works.theory_depth: The Intentional Fallacy argues that the poet's intentions are irrelevant to the poem's meaning, and that the poem's meaning can only be understood through close reading and formal analysis.

works.title: The Affective Fallacy
quotes: The affective fallacy is the confusion of the poem with its causes, or of the poem with its effects.
The poem itself cannot be verified or falsified.
works.theory_depth: The Affective Fallacy argues that the emotional response to a poem is not a reliable guide to its meaning, and that the poem's meaning can only be understood through close reading and formal analysis.

legacy.posthumous_notes: Their work has had a lasting impact on literary theory and criticism.
